dpv-ai|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#AISyste.note: ai systems vs concrete risk associated to an ai system

dpv-ai|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#DataVal.note: activity vs concept and concrete risk

dpv-aiact|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#Post.note: activity vs description of a concrete plan
activity vs description of a concrete system

dpv-aiact|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#AISy.note: ai system (concept) vs the ability to achieve an intended purpose

dpv|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#DataProces.note: activity vs description of a concrete policy
activity vs description of a concrete processing record
activity vs description of a concrete processing agreemant

ml-onto|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#ModelE.note: ml-onto class describes the SETTING of a specific value during evaluation, while aidoc describes the activity

ml-onto|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#Modali.note: aidoc describes the form in which the AI system is provided or placed on the market, while ml-onto (mlso) describes the different types or forms of data that exist

mlschema|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#Model.note: mlschema describes the class with a DISTINCT SETTING of an evaluation value, while aidoc describes the activity

rains|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#ModelEva.note: rains (mls) describes again a DISTINCT SETTING of a value, while aidoc describes the activity

rains|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#Log|http.note: aidoc describes "A record of system events, performance, and traceability." while rains describes "A specific type of planned sao:AccountableResult which represents a high level reference to logs of information pertaining either to Installation or Changes"

rains|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#AISystem.note: aidoc is broader, while rains describes an ai system with a specified accountability plan
aidoc describes a category of tasks or functions that an AI system can perform while rains describes an ai system for which an accountability plan is specified
aidoc specifies a concrete AI System while rains (sao) specifies a general system with collected accountability information

rains|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#DataClea.note: aidoc describes a procedure how data is cleaned, while rains describes a specific type of sao:InformationElement which records a specific piece of information detailing how the data is collected.
aidoc describes a procedure how data is cleaned, while rains describes a specific type of sao:InformationElement which records a specific piece of information detailing how the data is preprocessed.

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#Dataset|h.note: vair Dataset DOES NOT provide a description, but a close match can be assumed

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#Standard|.note: vair is narrower with Regulation (EU) No 1025/2012 on European standardisation as source, while aidoc is broader (harmonised standard)

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#DataValid.note: aidoc (activity) vs vair (data used to compare the performance of different candidate models.   [ISO/IEC 22989, 3.2.15])

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#DataTrain.note: aidoc activity vs vair (data used to train a machine learning model.  [ISO/IEC 22989, 3.3.16])

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#TrainingD.note: aidoc (data sheet focusing on training methodologies, techniques, hyperparameters and dataset usage for training, validation and testing.) vs vair data used to train a machine learning model.  [ISO/IEC 22989, 3.3.16]

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#MLPipelin.note: aidoc describes the whole pipeline while vair is specific about the process of optimizing of optimizing model parameters through computational techniques, such that the model's behaviour reflects the data or experience.

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#PostMarke.note: aidoc activity vs vair concrete plan as required by the EU AI Act

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#DataValid.note: activity vs concrete bias in validation data
activity vs concrete irrelevancies in validation data

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#DataTrain.note: activity vs concrete bias in validation data
activity vs concrete irrelevancies in validation data

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#DataValid.note: activity vs concrete incompleteness in validation data (concept)
activity vs concrete unrepresentatives in validation data
aidoc activity (EU AI Act 2024/1689, Article 3(30)) vs vair activity (ISO/IEC 22989:2022, 6.2.4)
activity vs concrete erroneous validation data

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#DataTrain.note: activity vs concrete incomplete training data

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#MLPipelin.note: activity vs libraries (software used for machine learning)

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#AIModel|h.note: aidoc describes a concrete AI/ML model while vair describes not only a model, but also a system, entity, phenomenon, process or data

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#DataTrain.note: activity vs concrete unrepresentative training data

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#MLPipelin.note: airo pipeline/activity vs vair (concrete) resource on which a ML model runs

vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#DataTrain.note: activity vs concrete erroneous training data

fn-band/dpv-ai|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap.note: activity vs concrete example of poisoning data
activity vs concrete example of outdated testing data

aidoc describes a computational representation that enables an AI method to execute an AI task, while dpv-ai describes a model that displays generality in terms of capabilities and potential applications according to the EU AI Act

aidoc describes a a category of tasks or functions that an AI system can perform, such as sensory, knowledge, or control action processing while dpv-ai describes AI systems intended to be more efficient, cost-effective, and accessible while maintaining or even improving their performance
activity vs concrete concept representing bias in testing data
activity vs concrete concept representing inconsitencies in testing data

aidoc describes a category of tasks or functions that an AI system can perform, such as sensory, knowledge, or control action processing while dpv-ai describes concrete risks associated with ai systems
activity vs concrete concept representing incomplete data in validation data

activity of managing revisions and tracking changes in model iterations vs process to determine or to improve the parameters of a machine learning model based on a machine learning technique by using training data

activity vs concrete input, which seems normal for a human but is wrongly classified by ML models (according to The EU Assessment List for Trustworthy AI (ALTAI),https://data.europa.eu/doi/10.2759/002360)
activity vs concrete concept representing validation data being unrepresentative
activity vs concrete concept representing testing data being sparse

fn-band/dpv-aiact|https://w3id.org/aidoc.note: activity vs documentation of the post-market monitoring system in place, as required by the AI Act

aidoc describes a computational representation that enables an AI method to execute an AI task, while dpv-ai describes a model that displays generality in terms of capabilities and potential applications according to the EU AI Act

fn-band/dpv-tech|https://w3id.org/aidoc-.note: activity of developing software vs concrete parts or components of the technology such as programs and data

A logical or physical software component of the AI system, such as a service, module, or microservice participating in the overall processing vs software that acts on behalf of another Entity or technology

fn-band/mcro|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#P.note: concrete metric vs section describing all metrics

fn-band/ml-onto|https://w3id.org/aidoc-a.note: concrete executable software realizing an algorithm vs software as a general concept

fn-band/prov-o|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap.note: A logical or physical software component of the AI system, such as a service, module, or microservice participating in the overall processing vs software that acts on behalf of another Entity or technology

fn-band/vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#P.note: activity vs documentation of the system in place

fn-band/vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#H.note: aidoc narrows to ai system while vair is the general concept

fn-band/vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#T.note: the training data sheet is narrower (contains more) than only incomplete training data

fn-band/vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#M.note: pipeline of activities vs concrete concept of a framework
activity of managing revisions vs type of attack
activities of the pipeline vs concrete algorithm in place

fn-band/vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#S.note: activity of managing revisions vs general software concept

fn-band/vair|https://w3id.org/aidoc-ap#M.note: broader activity of evaluation vs narrower re-evaluation based on results
